subheadline: Zergboard gives you a Trello-simple interface with Linear-level structure, multi-tenant workspaces, and an API-first core so your agent flows can create, update, and resolve work automatically.
value_proposition: A kanban project board that sits between Trello's simplicity and Linear's structure, with an API-first design built for both human teams and AI agent automation.
target_audience: Small-to-mid-size product and engineering teams — including AI/agent developers — who want structured project management without the overhead of enterprise tools.

pricing.price_anchoring: Anchored against implicit waste of enterprise tools; 'Start free, upgrade only when you grow' and 'Annual billing saves roughly 15%' reduce commitment anxiety.

features: Kanban cards and columns with fast drag-and-drop updates
Multi-tenant workspaces with organization-level isolation and membership controls
API-first architecture for agent flows, bots, and automation
Webhooks and API tokens for event-driven integrations
Superuser admin panel for platform-level metrics and governance
differentiators: Explicit positioning between Trello (simple) and Linear (structured): 'Trello-simple interface with Linear-level structure'
Agent/AI workflow as a first-class use case: 'your agent flows can create, update, and resolve work automatically'
Multi-tenant by default — not bolted on
Self-hostable on Fly.io with portable deployment primitives
'API-first core' framing vs. API as an afterthought
trust_signals: Self-hosting option signals openness and portability
Docs availability as static pages signals maturity

design_patterns.layout: Standard top-nav landing page: hero with subheadline and CTAs, live product preview snapshot, feature grid (4 cards), pricing table with monthly/yearly toggle, FAQ accordion
design_patterns.color_scheme: Cannot fully determine from text, but technical/dark aesthetic implied by product category and naming conventions
design_patterns.typography: Clean, functional — implied by minimal prose and short declarative copy
notable_patterns: Live kanban snapshot in hero simulating real-time board activity
Agent-generated activity blurb ('Agent flow updated 7 tickets from QA failures') as social proof substitute

emotional_tone: Confident and pragmatic — appeals to builders who are tired of bloated tools, not aspirational or warm
key_objections_addressed: Can AI agents actually use this? — Yes, API is 'intentionally minimal, stable, and built for automation-driven ticket flows'
Does it handle multiple clients or tenants? — Yes, isolated per organization with membership access control
Am I locked in? Can I self-host? — Yes, portable stack on Nuxt + Postgres
Is there a free option before I commit? — Yes, up to 3 users free
missing_or_weak: Zero social proof: no customer logos, testimonials, user counts, or press mentions — the single biggest credibility gap
No named customers or case studies showing agent workflows in production
Live board snapshot metrics (12 backlog, 6 in progress, 34 done) appear illustrative rather than real platform data — missed opportunity for authentic proof
No founder or team credibility signals (who built this, why trust it)
No security or compliance mentions (SOC 2, encryption, data residency) — likely a blocker for enterprise evaluation
The 'Built for teams and agents' superheadline is vague and undersells the agent-first angle
No integration ecosystem shown (GitHub, Slack, Jira, etc.) — limits perceived extensibility
Scale tier says 'Talk to sales' but no indication of what 'dedicated onboarding' or 'audit exports' actually means
standout_elements: Trello vs. Linear positioning is immediately legible and resonant for the target audience
Agent-first framing is genuinely differentiated — most PM tools treat automation as secondary
FAQ directly neutralizes the four most likely objections before they become drop-off
Pricing is transparent, ungated, and structured with a low-friction free entry point
Self-hosting mention earns trust with developer/infra-conscious buyers
Compact, scannable copy — no filler, high information density
